message.content:
This parent section (pp. 51–52) and its associated child analyses deliver a retrieval-optimized synthesis of 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, focusing on genre-specific user acquisition (UA) strategies, early-stage performance validation, and cross-market industry expansion.

**Topics & Entities:** Covers early metric validation, retention benchmarks, and scalability readiness for 4X/SLG titles; advertising and creative trend analysis across Strategy, RPG, Simulation, Casual, Puzzle, and Casino/Entertainment genres; and Chinese publisher overseas expansion dynamics. Key entities include Funtap Games, core genres (SLG, RTS, Idle, Roguelike, MMO, Hybrid Casual, Puzzle, Slots), platforms (Android/iOS, TikTok, Steam), analytics provider 广大大 (GuangdaDa), and notable publishers/titles (Tencent, Moonton, Turkish studios, *Whiteout Survival*, *Black Myth: Wukong*, *Gossip Harbor*).

**Dates:** Primary analytical window spans January–December 2025, with industry context referencing late 2020 (product model exploration), 2023 (testing/adjustment), 2024 (major releases), 2025 (current "quality leap"/strategic pivot), and 2026 (projected focus).

**Definitions:** FTUE (First Time User Experience), "honeymoon period" (initial engagement phase), "high-frequency, low-duration" session model, "social watershed" (retention divergence point), "10-hour rule" (600 cumulative minutes in week one increases paying conversion probability by 5x), LTV, "Adjustment" phase, "Traffic-driven" strategy, and "Numerical-value" design model.

**Procedures & Creative Formulas:** Details standardized ad sequences per genre: Strategy (high-altitude fall → number growth/parkour → clearance → intentional failure); RPG (AI intro → item showcase → battles → failure → customization → combo); Simulation (interactive choice → plot → failure → tragedy; or affair → problem → tug-of-war → failure); Casual (AI/viral opening → gameplay → showcase); Puzzle (micro-horror BGM → prop display → failure → real-world recreation → gameplay → download); Casino (player rage → staff apology → fake download; or beauty → gold showcase → gameplay). Operational procedures include hybrid FTUE within 15 minutes, mandatory alliance onboarding within 24 hours, Week 1 speed-up deployment to establish ~60-min daily habits, D1/D2 monetization prompts, early paid UA testing, and AI-driven voice/text replacement for cross-market creative adaptation.

**Claims:** Early metrics are mandatory to validate operational hook strategies; D7 retention depends heavily on social integration; number-gate parkour has replaced traditional formats as the primary casual SLG UA method; niche themes and realistic management mechanics drive higher engagement; entertaining content is the new baseline for ad openings; live-action drama creatives will dominate future casino UA; AI significantly improves cross-market adaptation efficiency; SLG remains the largest 2025 subcategory; and publishers are pivoting from experimental product models to established traffic-acquisition strategies.

**Metrics & Figures:** Features multiple monthly trend figures (Jan–Dec 2025) tracking advertiser volume and new creative launch ratios: Strategy (4.0K–5.5K advertisers, 77.2%–89.3% new creatives; 0–400 avg active, 53.0%–65.8% new); RPG (5.5K–7.5K advertisers, 74.3%–87.0% new); Simulation (monthly new creative rates: 41.7%–51.3%); Casual (~7.0K–10.0K advertisers, ~76.9%–86.8% new; 0–300 avg active, ~43.4%–53.5% new); Puzzle (0–40K advertisers, ~83.1%–89.2% new; 0–150 avg active, ~65.9%–76.1% new). Additional metrics include D1/D7 retention thresholds, the 5x conversion multiplier tied to the 10-hour rule, and a six-year overseas revenue milestone (>100B RMB).

**Evidence Types:** Sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, platform-derived monthly metric breakdowns, longitudinal trend visualizations, comparative genre performance data, heat/popularity rankings, industry analytics, and strategic market observations.

Procedures: Ad sequence (micro-horror BGM → prop display → failure → real-world prop → gameplay → download).     - *Node 0121 (Casino/Entertainment UA Trends):* Topics: Casino game ad strategies, cross-market trends. Entities: Slots, Looters, poker, bingo, GuangdaDa. Dates: Jan-Dec 2025. Procedures: Narrative 1 (player rage → staff apology → fake game → download), Narrative 2 (beauty → real gold → game showcase); AI voice/text replacement for cross-market. Claims: Live-action drama creatives future focus; AI boosts cross-market adaptation efficiency. Evidence: High-heat creative data, clickable video/image links. No specific quant metrics/tables.
    - *Node 0123 (Media Voices/Industry Trends):* Topics: Chinese mobile game overseas expansion (2023-2026), genre drivers (SLG, Merge/Hybrid casual), market consolidation, mini-game dynamics, PC/console. Entities: Tencent, TikTok, Moonton, Turkish studios, specific titles (Whiteout Survival, Black Myth: Wukong, etc.), Markets (US, JP, KR, EU, CN). Dates: Late 2020, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026, 6-year milestone (>100B RMB overseas revenue). response_text:
This parent section (pp. 51–52) and its associated child analyses deliver a retrieval-optimized synthesis of 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, focusing on genre-specific user acquisition (UA) strategies, early-stage performance validation, and cross-market industry expansion.

**Topics & Entities:** Covers early metric validation, retention benchmarks, and scalability readiness for 4X/SLG titles; advertising and creative trend analysis across Strategy, RPG, Simulation, Casual, Puzzle, and Casino/Entertainment genres; and Chinese publisher overseas expansion dynamics. Key entities include Funtap Games, core genres (SLG, RTS, Idle, Roguelike, MMO, Hybrid Casual, Puzzle, Slots), platforms (Android/iOS, TikTok, Steam), analytics provider 广大大 (GuangdaDa), and notable publishers/titles (Tencent, Moonton, Turkish studios, *Whiteout Survival*, *Black Myth: Wukong*, *Gossip Harbor*).

**Dates:** Primary analytical window spans January–December 2025, with industry context referencing late 2020 (product model exploration), 2023 (testing/adjustment), 2024 (major releases), 2025 (current "quality leap"/strategic pivot), and 2026 (projected focus).

**Definitions:** FTUE (First Time User Experience), "honeymoon period" (initial engagement phase), "high-frequency, low-duration" session model, "social watershed" (retention divergence point), "10-hour rule" (600 cumulative minutes in week one increases paying conversion probability by 5x), LTV, "Adjustment" phase, "Traffic-driven" strategy, and "Numerical-value" design model.

**Procedures & Creative Formulas:** Details standardized ad sequences per genre: Strategy (high-altitude fall → number growth/parkour → clearance → intentional failure); RPG (AI intro → item showcase → battles → failure → customization → combo); Simulation (interactive choice → plot → failure → tragedy; or affair → problem → tug-of-war → failure); Casual (AI/viral opening → gameplay → showcase); Puzzle (micro-horror BGM → prop display → failure → real-world recreation → gameplay → download); Casino (player rage → staff apology → fake download; or beauty → gold showcase → gameplay). Operational procedures include hybrid FTUE within 15 minutes, mandatory alliance onboarding within 24 hours, Week 1 speed-up deployment to establish ~60-min daily habits, D1/D2 monetization prompts, early paid UA testing, and AI-driven voice/text replacement for cross-market creative adaptation.

**Claims:** Early metrics are mandatory to validate operational hook strategies; D7 retention depends heavily on social integration; number-gate parkour has replaced traditional formats as the primary casual SLG UA method; niche themes and realistic management mechanics drive higher engagement; entertaining content is the new baseline for ad openings; live-action drama creatives will dominate future casino UA; AI significantly improves cross-market adaptation efficiency; SLG remains the largest 2025 subcategory; and publishers are pivoting from experimental product models to established traffic-acquisition strategies.

**Metrics & Figures:** Features multiple monthly trend figures (Jan–Dec 2025) tracking advertiser volume and new creative launch ratios: Strategy (4.0K–5.5K advertisers, 77.2%–89.3% new creatives; 0–400 avg active, 53.0%–65.8% new); RPG (5.5K–7.5K advertisers, 74.3%–87.0% new); Simulation (monthly new creative rates: 41.7%–51.3%); Casual (~7.0K–10.0K advertisers, ~76.9%–86.8% new; 0–300 avg active, ~43.4%–53.5% new); Puzzle (0–40K advertisers, ~83.1%–89.2% new; 0–150 avg active, ~65.9%–76.1% new). Additional metrics include D1/D7 retention thresholds, the 5x conversion multiplier tied to the 10-hour rule, and a six-year overseas revenue milestone (>100B RMB).

**Evidence Types:** Sourced from GuangdaDa’s Ad Creative and Advertiser Analysis modules, platform-derived monthly metric breakdowns, longitudinal trend visualizations, comparative genre performance data, heat/popularity rankings, industry analytics, and strategic market observations.
